mission.title: Install and Configure Volcano on Kubernetes
mission.description: Volcano is a Kubernetes-native batch scheduling system that enhances the capabilities of the standard kube-scheduler. It is designed to manage and optimize various batch and elastic workloads, making it ideal for AI, Big Data, and HPC applications.

steps.title: Add the Volcano Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Volcano Helm chart repository:

```bash
helm repo add volcano-sh https://volcano-sh.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Volcano using Helm
steps.description:
Install Volcano in the volcano-system namespace:

```bash
helm install volcano volcano-sh/volcano \
  --namespace volcano-system \
  --create-namespace
```

To install a specific version:
```bash
helm install volcano volcano-sh/volcano \
  --namespace volcano-system \
  --create-namespace \
  --version 1.12.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that all Volcano components are running:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n volcano-system
```

You should see pods for the volcano-scheduler, volcano-controller-manager, and volcano-admission running.

steps.title: Verify CRDs are installed
steps.description:
Check that Volcano CRDs are registered:

```bash
kubectl get crd | grep volcano.sh
```

You should see CRDs like jobs.batch.volcano.sh, queues.scheduling.volcano.sh, and podgroups.scheduling.volcano.sh.

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Volcano release:

```bash
helm uninstall volcano --namespace volcano-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove the Volcano CRDs:

```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep volcano.sh | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Delete the namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the volcano-system namespace:

```bash
kubectl delete namespace volcano-system
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Volcano
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade:

```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade volcano volcano-sh/volcano --namespace volcano-system
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Volcano pods:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n volcano-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability and scheduling issues:

```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n volcano-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Volcano controller not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Volcano controller pod fails to start, check the logs:

```bash
kubectl logs -l app=volcano-controller -n volcano-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Volcano scheduler not scheduling jobs
troubleshooting.description:
Check the scheduler logs for errors:

```bash
kubectl logs -l app=volcano-scheduler -n volcano-system
```

Also verify that jobs are using the volcano scheduler:
```bash
kubectl get vcjob -A
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Volcano will have the scheduler, controller-manager, and admission pods running in the volcano-system namespace. You can then submit Volcano Jobs (vcjob) and use Volcano queues for batch workload scheduling.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm v3 and kubectl installed.
